context_pointers.vague_reference: use the same parameter extraction logic as the form handler
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The code must follow the exact pattern of N/N+1 value handling found in the existing form integration module

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_1_multipart_file_fields.json.description: Parse a multipart/form-data body (provided either as a raw string or Base64-encoded) and expose the uploaded file part to the handler.

event.body:
------WebKitFormBoundaryDP6Z1qHQSzB6Pf8c
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="uploadFile1"; filename="test.txt"
Content-Type: text/plain

Hello World!
------WebKitFormBoundaryDP6Z1qHQSzB6Pf8c--

cases.expected_output:
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"uploadFile1":{"fieldname":"uploadFile1","filename":"test.txt","encoding":"7bit","mimetype":"text/plain","content":"Hello World!"}}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=132
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"uploadFile1":{"fieldname":"uploadFile1","filename":"test.txt","encoding":"7bit","mimetype":"text/plain","content":"Hello World!"}}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=132
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

feature10_2_multipart_value_field.json.description: Parse a multipart/form-data text field and expose its decoded value to the handler.

cases.expected_output:
[response] status=200 isBase64Encoded=false body={"html":{"fieldname":"html","encoding":"7bit","mimetype":"text/plain","value":"

Hello World

"}} header.connection=keep-alive header.content-length=100 header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8 header.date=

feature1_request_body.json.description: Decode an incoming request body and forward it to the routed handler, computing the Content-Length header; supports both a plain UTF-8 body and a Base64-encoded body.

cases.expected_output:
[request]
request.headers.content-type=application/json
request.headers.content-length=14
request.body={"greet":"hi"}
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world2"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=18
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

[request]
request.headers.content-type=application/json
request.headers.content-length=15
request.body={"greet":"hi"}
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world2"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=18
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

feature2_1_path_resolution.json.description: Choose the path the framework routes to: prefer the explicit request path, and fall back to the raw path (deriving the method from the request context) when only that is present.

cases.expected_output:
[request]
request.url=/test
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

[request]
request.url=/prod/projects?t=1698604776681
request.query.t=1698604776681
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

feature2_2_path_parameter_as_path.json.description: When configured to use a named path parameter as the path, route to that parameter value instead of the full request/raw path, for both proxy-integration event shapes.

cases.expected_output:
[request]
request.url=/projects?t=1698604776681
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

[request]
request.url=/projects?t=1698604776681
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

feature2_3_stage_handling.json.description: Strip the API stage prefix from the path by default so handlers are stage-agnostic; retain the stage prefix when explicitly requested.

cases.expected_output:
[request]
request.url=/test
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

[request]
request.url=/dev/test
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

feature3_1_multi_value_query.json.description: Expose multi-valued query string parameters to the handler as arrays of values.

cases.expected_output:
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"foo":["qux","bar"]}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=21
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

feature3_2_comma_separated_query.json.description: For protocol version 2.0, optionally split comma-separated query values into arrays; a toggle controls whether the split happens.

cases.expected_output:
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"foo":["qux","bar"]}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=21
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"foo":"qux,bar"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

feature3_3_elb_url_decoding.json.description: For load-balancer sourced events, URL-decode both query keys and query values (single and multi-valued).

cases.expected_output:
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"q$":"foo?bar"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=16
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"q$":["foo@bar","foo?bar"]}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=28
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

feature3_4_query_no_decode.json.description: For non load-balancer events, query values are passed through verbatim, without URL-decoding.

cases.expected_output:
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"foo":["foo%40bar","foo%3Fbar"]}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=33
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

feature3_5_retain_event_query.json.description: Splitting comma-separated values for the handler must not mutate the original event's query parameters, which remain readable through request decoration regardless of the split toggle.

cases.expected_output:
[request]
request.awsLambdaEvent.queryStringParameters={"foo":"qux,bar"}
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"foo":["qux","bar"]}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=21
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

[request]
request.awsLambdaEvent.queryStringParameters={"foo":"qux,bar"}
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"foo":"qux,bar"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

feature4_1_cookies_to_cookie_header.json.description: Join the protocol v2 cookie list into a single Cookie request header visible to the handler.

cases.expected_output:
[request]
request.headers.cookie=foo=bar
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

feature4_2_multi_value_headers.json.description: Promote a header to the request only when it carries more than one value; a multi-header with a single value is not promoted, and a header with several values is exposed as the combined value.

cases.expected_output:
[request]
request.headers.x-multi=just-the-first,and-the-second
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

[request]
request.headers.x-custom-multi-bad=<undefined>
request.headers.x-custom-multi-gut=100,200
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

feature4_3_serialize_event_header.json.description: Optionally serialize the original invocation event into a URL-encoded request header so handlers can inspect the raw event; the header is absent when the feature is off (the default).

cases.expected_output:
[request]
request.headers.x-apigateway-event=%7B%22version%22%3A%222.0%22%2C%22httpMethod%22%3A%22GET%22%2C%22path%22%3A%22%2Ftest%22%2C%22headers%22%3A%7B%22X-My-Header%22%3A%22wuuusaaa%22%7D%7D
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

[request]
request.headers.x-apigateway-event=<undefined>
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

feature5_1_response_json_cookies_v2.json.description: Render a JSON handler response into a proxy result; for protocol v2, Set-Cookie headers are returned as a dedicated cookie list rather than response headers.

cases.expected_output:
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>
cookies=qwerty=one,qwerty=two

feature5_2_response_v1_cookies_and_joine.description: For protocol v1 (no version field), Set-Cookie values are returned under a multi-value headers map; other multi-valued response headers are comma-joined into a single header value.

cases.expected_output:
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world2"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=18
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>
header.x-custom-header=ciao,salve
multiValueHeaders.set-cookie=qwerty=one,qwerty=two

feature5_3_response_empty_and_coercion.j.description: An empty handler response yields an empty body with zero Content-Length; non-string response header values are coerced to strings; removing Content-Type omits it from the result.

cases.expected_output:
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body=
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=0
header.date=<present>
header.x-non-string=1717171717171
multiValueHeaders.set-cookie=qwerty=one,qwerty=two

feature6_1_binary_mime_types.json.description: When the response Content-Type matches a configured binary MIME type, the body is Base64-encoded and flagged as such.

cases.expected_output:
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=true
body=SGVsbG8gYmluYXJ5IHBheWxvYWQh
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=21
header.content-type=application/octet-stream
header.date=<present>

feature6_2_content_encoding_base64.json.description: A compressed response (non-identity Content-Encoding) is Base64-encoded by default even without a configured binary MIME type.

cases.expected_output:
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=true
body=SGVsbG8gYmluYXJ5IHBheWxvYWQh
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-encoding=br
header.content-length=21
header.content-type=application/octet-stream
header.date=<present>

feature6_3_custom_base64_predicate.json.description: A caller-supplied predicate decides whether a response should be Base64-encoded based on the response headers.

cases.expected_output:
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=true
body=SGVsbG8gYmluYXJ5IHBheWxvYWQh
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=21
header.content-type=application/octet-stream
header.date=<present>
header.x-base64-encoded=1

feature7_1_request_decoration.json.description: Decorate the request so the handler can read the original invocation event and the original invocation context.

cases.expected_output:
[request]
request.awsLambdaEvent.httpMethod=GET
request.awsLambdaContext={"functionName":"fn"}
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

feature7_2_decoration_in_hook.json.description: The request decoration is available in lifecycle hooks that run before the handler, not only inside the handler.

cases.expected_output:
[request]
onRequest.awsLambdaEvent.httpMethod=GET
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>

feature8_1_callback_invocation.json.description: Support node-style callback invocation: the proxy delivers the same proxy result through a callback as it does through a promise.

cases.expected_output:
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>
cookies=qwerty=one,qwerty=two

feature8_2_callback_waits_option.json.description: When provided, the empty-event-loop-wait flag is written onto the invocation context before the request is processed.

cases.expected_output:
[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>
[context]
context.callbackWaitsForEmptyEventLoop=false

[response]
status=200
isBase64Encoded=false
body={"hello":"world"}
header.connection=keep-alive
header.content-length=17
header.content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
header.date=<present>
[context]
context.callbackWaitsForEmptyEventLoop=true

feature9_error_resilience.json.description: If the underlying request processing fails unexpectedly, the proxy returns a 500 result with an empty body and no headers instead of leaking the failure, for both promise and callback invocation.

cases.expected_output:
[response]
status=500
isBase64Encoded=<undefined>
body=

[response]
status=500
isBase64Encoded=<undefined>
body=

_constraints.input_condition: Domain structure is 'protocol v2', and the input structure contains 'queryStringParameters', 'multiValueQueryStringParameters', and 'headers' keys
_constraints.output_rule: The Handler must use the values from 'multiValueQueryStringParameters' to construct the query object

hide.replacement: a specific protocol version string — verify with the API spec

vague_refs.vague_phrase: use the same parameter extraction logic as the form handler
vague_refs.actual_meaning: The code must follow the exact pattern of N/N+1 value handling found in the existing form integration module
